acceptedQuestions.subject: Science
acceptedQuestions.prompt:
With reference to stellar evolution and black holes, consider the following statements:

1. Low- and medium-mass stars evolve into white dwarfs at the end of their life cycle.
2. Both high-mass and very high-mass stars undergo a supernova explosion before reaching their final evolutionary stages.
3. Black holes are classified solely into stellar-mass and supermassive categories based on their mass.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Low- and medium-mass stars (like the Sun) evolve into White Dwarfs. Statement 2 is correct: High-mass stars evolve into Neutron Stars after a supernova explosion, and very high-mass stars evolve into Black Holes after a supernova explosion. Statement 3 is incorrect: Black holes are classified into stellar-mass, intermediate-mass (IMBH), and supermassive black holes.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: Stellar evolution is determined by mass. While low/medium-mass stars become white dwarfs, heavier stars undergo supernova explosions to become either neutron stars or black holes. Black holes exist in at least three mass categories: stellar, intermediate, and supermassive.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Low- and medium-mass stars (like the Sun) evolve into White Dwarfs. High-mass stars evolve into Neutron Stars after a supernova explosion. Very high-mass stars evolve into Black Holes after a supernova explosion. Types of black holes include Stellar-Mass, Intermediate-Mass (IMBH), and Supermassive Black Holes.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Good conceptual coverage of stellar evolution and black hole classification. The distractor in statement 3 is plausible and tests attention to detail.

reviewerNotes: All three questions were retained but refined for better UPSC alignment. Candidate 3 was updated to the modern Statement-I/Statement-II format instead of the older Assertion-Reason style. The distractors and explanations are tight and directly supported by the text.
